name: Samuel Coleridge-Taylor
abstract: Samuel Coleridge-Taylor (15 August 1875 – 1 September 1912) was a British composer and conductor. He was particularly known for his three cantatas on the epic 1855 poem The Song of Hiawatha by American Henry Wadsworth Longfellow.

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur Briggs
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee confirms Gwendolyn Coleridge-Taylor was the pianist on a program featuring works by Coleridge-Taylor, but no personal interaction or relationship with the composer is described.

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: Did you ever hear of Coleridge S. Taylor? This was a colored composer. He composed 'Hiawatha's Wedding Feast' ... I (unintelligible) first time I'd ever heard anything like that
